meta.title: Claude Opus 4.7 API | APIDot
meta.description: Access Claude Opus 4.7 instantly on APIDot for frontier agentic coding, 1M context, 128K output, high-resolution vision, and production-ready dual chat endpoint support.

hero.title: Claude Opus 4.7 API Playground
hero.description: Test `claude-opus-4-7` on APIDot with either `/v1/chat/completions` or `/v1/messages`. Run coding, reasoning, and high-resolution vision workflows with the same production model id and transparent token pricing.

overview.title: Use Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot for frontier coding, reasoning, and long-context work
paragraphs: Claude Opus 4.7 is Anthropic's most capable generally available model for agentic coding, long-horizon reasoning, and complex professional workflows. On APIDot, you can access the production model id `claude-opus-4-7` instantly without switching platforms, while keeping pricing and request shape visible on one page.

Compared with earlier Opus releases, Claude Opus 4.7 is positioned around stronger coding thoroughness, better self-verification, improved tool use, and materially better vision for screenshots, charts, technical diagrams, and document-heavy tasks. The official launch material and current PoYo page both emphasize coding quality, long-running agent reliability, and high-resolution image understanding as the main reasons to adopt it.

This page is intentionally structured for production teams: a working playground, API examples, pricing that matches the current PoYo benchmark, and SEO content that explains where Claude Opus 4.7 fits best. If you need both OpenAI-compatible integration and Anthropic-native compatibility, APIDot exposes both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages` workflows around the same model.

capabilities.title: What Claude Opus 4.7 API is best at

items.title: Frontier agentic coding and code review
items.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is designed for multi-step engineering work, including large refactors, deep debugging, structured planning, and code review tasks where the model needs to verify its own reasoning before answering.

items.title: 1M context with 128K output
items.description: Use Claude Opus 4.7 API for repository-scale prompts, long document sets, and multi-file technical analysis. The 1M token context window and 128K output ceiling make it viable for demanding production workflows, not just short chat exchanges.

items.title: High-resolution vision up to 2576px / 3.75MP
items.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is the first Claude model with materially improved image resolution support. That makes it stronger for screenshot QA, dashboard reviews, chart interpretation, dense document parsing, and UI analysis tasks that break weaker multimodal models.

items.title: Dual endpoint compatibility for production migration
items.description: Use `/v1/chat/completions` when you want OpenAI-style integration, SDK compatibility, or a faster migration path from existing chat stacks. Use `/v1/messages` when you want the Anthropic-native request shape. APIDot supports both around the same Claude Opus 4.7 model id.

useCases.title: Who benefits from Claude Opus 4.7 API
useCases.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is a strong fit when accuracy, long context, and multi-step task execution matter more than raw lowest-cost chat output.
items: AI coding agents for multi-file engineering work
Repository analysis, code review, and migration planning
Long-context research, legal, finance, and knowledge workflows
High-resolution screenshot, chart, and document analysis
Internal copilots that need dual endpoint compatibility
Teams upgrading from earlier Opus or mixed provider chat stacks

quickstart.title: Start using Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot in three steps

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an APIDot API key and keep it on the server side. The same key can be used for chat, image, video, and audio models across your environment.

steps.title: Top up credits with token pricing in mind
steps.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is priced at $4 per 1M input tokens and $20 per 1M output tokens on this page, aligned to the current PoYo model page. Because the model can reason deeply and produce longer completions, monitor token usage rather than assuming flat per-request cost.

steps.title: Choose the endpoint that matches your stack
steps.description: Use `/v1/chat/completions` for OpenAI-compatible chat integration, or `/v1/messages` for Anthropic-native payloads. Keep the same model id `claude-opus-4-7` in both cases.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about Claude Opus 4.7 API

items.question: What is Claude Opus 4.7?
items.answer: Claude Opus 4.7 is Anthropic's most capable generally available model as of its April 16, 2026 launch. It is positioned for frontier coding, long-horizon reasoning, high-resolution vision, and demanding professional workflows.

items.question: How much does Claude Opus 4.7 cost on APIDot?
items.answer: Pricing on this page is aligned with the current PoYo reference: $4 per 1M input tokens and $20 per 1M output tokens for `claude-opus-4-7`.

items.question: Does APIDot support both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages`?
items.answer: Yes. This page exposes Claude Opus 4.7 through both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages`, so you can choose OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-native integration without changing the model id.

items.question: What makes Claude Opus 4.7 different from Opus 4.6?
items.answer: The main positioning shift is stronger agentic coding, better self-verification, improved tool use, and high-resolution vision support up to 2576px / 3.75MP. That makes Claude Opus 4.7 more attractive for engineering agents and document-heavy multimodal workflows.

items.question: Does Claude Opus 4.7 support long context?
items.answer: Yes. Claude Opus 4.7 supports a 1M token context window and up to 128K output tokens, making it viable for very long code, document, and analysis prompts.

items.question: Why can real-world cost feel higher than list price?
items.answer: Claude Opus 4.7 can use more tokens than simpler models because of deeper reasoning and longer outputs. Public launch materials also note tokenizer changes, so production teams should watch prompt and completion token usage rather than budgeting only from headline pricing.

items.question: When should I choose Claude Opus 4.7 over a cheaper chat model?
items.answer: Choose Claude Opus 4.7 when your workload depends on coding quality, long-context reliability, structured reasoning, and high-resolution multimodal understanding. For lightweight or cost-sensitive chat, a smaller model may be a better fit.
